The Company notes that 2,180,000 ordinary shares in the Company (prior to the Share Capital Reorganisation announced on 10 February 2026) (the "
Sanctioned Shares
") are held by a shareholder who is currently subject to applicable sanctions (the "
Sanctioned Shareholder
"). While the Sanctioned Shareholder remains subject to such sanctions, the Board has taken the view that the Company should not recognise the exercise of voting rights attaching to the Sanctioned Shares. Accordingly, the Company has not recognised, and would not take into account, any votes purportedly cast in respect of the Sanctioned Shares in relation to the resolutions put to the AGM or any shareholder votes to be held at General Meetings or Annual General Meetings in the future.
If, in due course, the Sanctioned Shareholder ceases to be subject to applicable sanctions (or the relevant restrictions otherwise no longer apply) , the Board currently intends to propose an appropriate resolution at the Company's next Annual General Meeting (or, if the Board considers it necessary or appropriate, at a general meeting) to seek shareholder approval to implement the Share Capital Reorganisation in relation to the Sanctioned Shares as the Sanctioned Shares were excluded from the Share Capital Reorganisation. Any such proposal would be announced separately in due course.

latest.title: Successful Completion of Bond Restructuring
latest.content:
Today 10:17
RNS Number : 1275I
Sound Energy PLC
12 June 2026
12 June 2026
Sound Energy PLC
​
("Sound Energy", the "Company" and together with its subsidiary undertakings the "Group")
Successful Completion of Bond Restructuring
Sound Energy, the AIM-listed transition energy company, is pleased to announce that, further to its announcements on 26 May 2026 and 28 May 2026, at the meeting of the holders (the "Noteholders") of the Company's Luxembourg listed EUR 28.8m 5.0% senior secured notes (the "Notes") held earlier today to consider the proposal for the restructuring of the Notes (the "Proposal"), the Proposal was duly approved by Noteholders by the requisite majority.
At the Noteholder meeting, quorum was reached with voting instructions representing EUR 24.3 million of the Notes lodged by Noteholders, with 96.30% of votes cast in favour of the Proposal.
As a result of the Noteholders' approval of the Proposal, the Notes may now be redeemed by the Company in accordance with the amended Condition 7.2A (
Special redemption at the option of the Issuer
), details of which are set out in the Consent Solicitation Memorandum (CSM) published on 28 May 2026.
Notice of the result of the Noteholder meeting will shortly be sent to Noteholders and will be available on the Company's website at
www.soundenergyplc.com
thereafter.
Andrew Matharu, Chief Financial Officer, commented "We are pleased that Noteholders have supported the proposed amendments to the Eurobonds, which subject to amended Condition 7.2A, will enable the Company to significantly reduce its balance sheet debt."
For further information please visit
www.soundenergyplc.com

latest.title: Notice of GM
latest.content:
Today 14:32
RNS Number : 2963G
Sound Energy PLC
29 May 2026
29 May 2026
Sound Energy PLC
​
("Sound Energy", the "Company" and together with its subsidiary undertakings the "Group")
Notice of General Meeting
Sound Energy PLC (AIM: SOU), the AIM quoted transition energy company, announces that a Shareholder Circular containing a notice convening a General Meeting of the Company with proposals in relation to the conditional sale of Sound Energy Meridja (SEML) is available on the Company's website at www.soundenergyplc.com and has been posted to shareholders today.
The General Meeting is to be held at 10.00 a.m. on 16 June 2026 at Sound Energy plc, 20 St Dunstan's Hill, London EC3R 8HL.
Proposed Disposal of Sound Energy Meridja Limited
On 26 May 2026 Sound Energy PLC announced it had entered into a binding Sale and Purchase Agreement (the "SPA") for the divestment of the Company's interests in the Tendrara Exploitation Concession (20%), onshore Morocco, by way of the disposal of the entire share capital of Sound Energy Merijda Limited ("SEML") to Managem SA ("Managem"). The Company will receive a total of US$57 million (subject to working capital adjustments) by way of a US$1 consideration for the shares and the repayment of loans made by the Company to SEML.
In view of the relative size of the disposal of SEML to the Company, the disposal is deemed to result in a fundamental change of the business of the Company for the purpose of Rule 15 of the AIM Rules and it is therefore conditional upon the approval of Shareholders.
As the disposal will result in the Company divesting of all, or substantially all, of its existing trading business, activities and assets, the Company will be deemed to become an AIM Rule 15 Cash Shell following Completion of the disposal and will be required to make an acquisition or acquisitions which constitutes a reverse takeover under AIM Rule 14 within six months of completion.
In the event the Company does not complete a reverse takeover under AIM Rule 14 within such six-month period or seek readmission to trading on AIM as an investing company pursuant to Aim Rule 8, the Company's ordinary shares would be suspended from trading pursuant to AIM Rule 40. Thereafter, if such a transaction has not been completed within a further six-month period, admission to trading on AIM of the Company's ordinary shares would be cancelled.
Completion of the proposed disposal is conditional,
inter alia
, on the Shareholders passing the Ordinary Resolution being proposed at the General Meeting. If the Shareholders do not pass the Resolution, Completion of the Disposal will not proceed.
EXPECTED TIMETABLE OF PRINCIPAL EVENTS
Publication and posting to Shareholders of this document
29 May 2026
Latest time and date for receipt of Forms of Proxy
10.00 am on 12 June 2026
General Meeting
10.00 a.m. on 16 June 2026
Announcement of results of General Meeting
16 June 2026
Estimated completion of the disposal
31 July 2026

latest.title: Publication of Annual Report and Notice of AGM
latest.content:
28 May 2026 09:32
RNS Number : 0600G
Sound Energy PLC
28 May 2026
28 May 2026
Sound Energy plc
("Sound Energy" or the "Company")
Publication of Annual Report and Notice of AGM
Sound Energy,
announces that its Annual Report and Accounts for the year ended 31 December 2025 has today been published, together with a notice convening the Company's annual general meeting (the "AGM").
The AGM is to be held at 20 St Dunstan's Hill, London, EC3R 8HL on Monday, 22 June 2026 at 2:00pm.
Copies of the Company's Annual Report and Notice of AGM are available on the Company's website at
www.soundenergyplc.com
.
In order to ensure that shareholders who cannot attend in person are able to follow the proceedings of the AGM, the Company will provide access online through the Investor Meet Company platform. However, shareholders will not be able to vote online during the Meeting and are therefore urged to submit their votes via proxy as early as possible.
Following the conclusion of the AGM, the Company will present an overview of its activities. This will be live streamed through the Investor meet platform. Shareholders are invited to pre submit questions to the Company in advance of the presentation via the Investor Meet Company Platform up to 9.00am on the 19th June 2026, being the day before the AGM and Presentation. Shareholders who wish to attend the AGM and presentation proceedings online should register for the meeting in advance via the following Investor Meet link:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/sound-energy-plc/register-investor
For further information please visit

company_30s.what_they_do: SOU.L is a small‑cap energy company that explores for and develops oil and gas assets.  It focuses on acquiring and improving low‑margin fields and selling parts of its portfolio to free cash for new projects.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global energy transition is pushing companies to find cheaper, cleaner supplies.  SOU’s strategy of buying undervalued assets and selling non‑core holdings can create value for investors if it lands on the right deals, especially in a market where asset prices are still volatile.
company_30s.current_state: After a period of heavy debt and a sale of its SEML exploration rights, the company is trimming its balance sheet and aiming for a leaner operation.  The stock has slid sharply, but the recent bond restructuring and AGM suggest the firm is trying to stabilize its finances.  It trades well below its pre‑crash valuation, with a price that sits at roughly a third of the last all‑time high.

rns_narrative: SOU has been busy cleaning up its balance sheet – a bond restructuring completed in mid‑June and the sale of the SEML assets earlier in May cut debt and free cash.  The company is also gearing up for an AGM and an investor presentation that will outline new drilling plans and potential financing.  In short, the narrative is one of a company trying to survive by reducing debt, selling non‑core assets, and pitching a clearer growth path to shareholders.

Three themes emerge: (1) **Debt relief** – the bond deal and asset sale reduce leverage; (2) **Strategic clarity** – AGM and investor talks promise a focused drilling programme; (3) **Cash‑flow pressure** – the company still needs to secure new capital to fund upcoming projects.

While the company is not yet profitable, the steps taken indicate an attempt to stop the cash burn and position itself for a turnaround.  The market’s silence around these moves heightens the surprise potential.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent optimism – investors are quiet but expect a rebound after the debt cleanup.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter focuses on the CEO’s confidence, the successful bond restructuring, and the promise of new exploration projects.  Several posts praise the company’s clean balance sheet and hint that a fresh funding round could be on the horizon.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bear voices worry that the company still lacks a clear revenue stream, that the drilling plans are unproven, and that the AGM could reveal more cash‑flow problems.  A few posts fear that the debt relief is only temporary and that the firm might face another restructuring.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from cautious skepticism to cautious optimism.  As the AGM approaches, the community is watching for any sign that the company can secure new capital or deliver a positive operational update.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no recent search spikes, meaning the market is largely unaware of the upcoming catalysts.

near_term_catalysts: The AGM on 28 May will set the next capital‑raising agenda; a positive outcome could lift the stock.
The investor presentation scheduled for early June will detail new drilling plans and projected cash flows.
Completion of the bond restructuring in mid‑June should reduce interest expenses and improve the balance sheet.
Any announcement of a new exploration licence or a drilling contract would be a clear trigger for a bounce.
Technical support near the 30‑day moving average could act as a floor; a sustained break above that level would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.watch_for: Monitor the AGM minutes for any new financing commitments, keep an eye on the volume spike around the investor presentation, and watch for a price bounce off the 30‑day MA.  A move above the 200‑day MA would be a strong confirmation of a longer‑term recovery.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a capitulation cycle – it has dropped steeply and the volume has thinned, suggesting the worst may be over.  The APEX compression score indicates a tight spring, so a rebound is likely if a catalyst materialises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company is 70% below its peak, giving a 66% upside if it recovers to 50% of the all‑time high.  A deep crash in a small‑cap energy firm is a classic contrarian set‑up; the bond restructuring and asset sale reduce leverage, while the AGM could unlock new capital.  Historically, such deep bottoms have produced large rebounds when smart money starts to accumulate.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The firm still has no proven revenue, relies on future drilling, and faces regulatory and financing uncertainty.  If the AGM fails to secure funding or if drilling results are poor, the price could stay near the current level or decline further, wiping out the upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Upside: 66% from the current low to 50% of peak.  Downside: up to 100% if the stock collapses to zero, but the risk of a total wipe is mitigated by the debt cleanup and the likely support at the 30‑day MA.  The asymmetry favours a contrarian long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a break above the 30‑day moving average with a small volume spike, allocating 2% of the portfolio.  Place a stop just below the 30‑day MA to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the AGM outcome or the investor presentation to confirm new capital.  A price rise to the 200‑day MA or a 5‑day volume spike would be a clear trigger.
playbook.if_passing: If the AGM reveals a debt‑repayment plan that requires a fresh capital raise and the company fails to secure it, consider exiting or reducing exposure.

ai_insights.bottom_line: SOU.L’s 70% crash, coupled with a debt‑reduction catalyst and silent market, creates a high‑reward contrarian play that could rebound to mid‑peak levels if the AGM unlocks new financing.
